quiz_questions.question_text: Which set of ordered pairs is a function? {(1,2), (3,4), (5,6)}

options.text: It is a function because every input has exactly one output
It is not a function
It depends on the context
It is only a relation

quiz_questions.explanation: Each input (1, 3, 5) maps to exactly one output, so this set is a function.

quiz_questions.question_text: The vertical line test determines if a graph represents:

options.text: A relation
A function
A one-to-one function
An equation

quiz_questions.explanation: If no vertical line intersects the graph at more than one point, the graph represents a function.

options.text: It is one-to-one
Yes, because outputs can repeat
Yes, it is a function
No, input 1 maps to both 2 and 5

quiz_questions.explanation: Input 1 has two different outputs (2 and 5), which violates the definition of a function.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of these is a function of x? (a) y = x²  (b) x = y²  (c) y = ±√x

options.text: All of them
x = y²
y = x²
y = ±√x

quiz_questions.explanation: y = x² gives one y for each x. Both x = y² and y = ±√x produce two y-values for some x-values.

quiz_questions.question_text: Every function is a relation. True or false?

options.text: Only for linear functions
Only sometimes
True
False

quiz_questions.explanation: Functions are a special subset of relations where every input has exactly one output. Every function qualifies as a relation, but not every relation is a function.

quiz_questions.question_text: A circle's graph fails the vertical line test because:

options.text: It's not a relation
It has no y-values
It actually passes the test
Vertical lines cross it at two points

quiz_questions.explanation: At most x-values within the circle's domain, a vertical line intersects the circle at two points (one above and one below the center).

options.text: Not a function
One-to-one
A function (every input is unique, even though outputs repeat)
Not a relation

quiz_questions.explanation: Each input (2, 4, 6) maps to exactly one output. Repeated outputs are allowed in a function; only repeated inputs with different outputs would fail.

quiz_questions.question_text: f(x) = x³ is:

options.text: A function AND one-to-one
Not a relation
A function but not one-to-one
Not a function

quiz_questions.explanation: Each input gives one output (function), and each output comes from exactly one input (one-to-one), since the cube function is strictly increasing.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a 'relation' in mathematics?

options.text: A type of equation that can be solved
A connection between two equations
A mapping where each input has one output
Any set of ordered pairs

quiz_questions.explanation: A relation is any set of ordered pairs (x, y). It may or may not be a function.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the defining property of a 'function'?

options.text: The relation must be a straight line
Inputs and outputs are always different
Every output has exactly one input
Every input has exactly one output

quiz_questions.explanation: A function is a relation in which each element of the domain (input) is paired with exactly one element of the range (output).

quiz_questions.question_text: What does the 'vertical line test' check?

options.text: Whether a graph represents a function by checking if any vertical line intersects it more than once
Whether the graph has a y-intercept
Whether the graph is a straight line
Whether the function is increasing

quiz_questions.explanation: The vertical line test states that a graph represents a function if and only if no vertical line crosses the graph at more than one point.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'one-to-one function' mean?

options.text: A function that has only one input and one output
A function where no two different inputs produce the same output
A function whose graph is a single line
A function that passes only the vertical line test

quiz_questions.explanation: A one-to-one (injective) function means that if f(a) = f(b), then a = b. No two different inputs share the same output.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does the 'horizontal line test' determine?

options.text: Whether a graph is a function
Whether a function is continuous
Whether a function is even or odd
Whether a function is one-to-one

quiz_questions.explanation: The horizontal line test checks if a function is one-to-one: if no horizontal line intersects the graph more than once, the function is one-to-one.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the 'domain' of a function?

options.text: The equation that defines the function
The graph of the function
The set of all output values
The set of all valid input values

quiz_questions.explanation: The domain is the complete set of all possible input values (x-values) for which the function is defined.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the 'range' of a function?

options.text: The interval where the function is increasing
The set of all input values
The difference between the maximum and minimum values
The set of all actual output values

quiz_questions.explanation: The range is the set of all actual output values (y-values) that the function produces.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a 'mapping diagram'?

options.text: A visual representation showing arrows from inputs to their corresponding outputs
A flowchart showing the steps to evaluate a function
A table of x and y values
A coordinate graph showing a function

quiz_questions.explanation: A mapping diagram displays domain elements on one side and range elements on the other, with arrows showing which inputs map to which outputs.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does it mean for a function to be 'onto' (surjective)?

options.text: The function passes the vertical line test
The function is one-to-one
Every element of the codomain is mapped to by at least one input
Every input is used exactly once

quiz_questions.explanation: A surjective (onto) function means every element in the codomain has at least one element in the domain that maps to it.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the difference between a 'function' and a 'one-to-one function'?

options.text: A one-to-one function must be linear
There is no difference
A function requires each input to have one output; a one-to-one function additionally requires each output to come from only one input
A function allows repeated inputs; a one-to-one function does not

quiz_questions.explanation: Every one-to-one function is a function, but a one-to-one function adds the requirement that no two different inputs produce the same output.

quiz_questions.question_text: A vending machine assigns each button (A1, A2, B1, B2) to exactly one snack. Is this relationship a function from buttons to snacks?

options.text: No, because two buttons could give the same snack
Yes, because each button gives exactly one snack
No, because it is a machine, not a function
Only if every snack is different

quiz_questions.explanation: Each input (button) maps to exactly one output (snack), so it is a function. Two buttons giving the same snack is allowed; only one button giving two different snacks would violate the function definition.

quiz_questions.question_text: A school assigns each student exactly one locker number. However, some lockers are shared by two students. Is the assignment from students to lockers a function?

options.text: No, because lockers are shared
It is a relation but not a function
Yes, because each student is assigned exactly one locker
Only if no lockers are shared

quiz_questions.explanation: Since each student (input) maps to exactly one locker (output), this is a function. The fact that two students share a locker means it is not one-to-one, but it is still a function.

quiz_questions.question_text: A GPS device gives the position (x, y) at each time t. At time t = 5 seconds, it shows positions (3, 4) and (3, 7) depending on the route. Is position a function of time?

options.text: No, because the same input t = 5 gives two different outputs
It depends on the speed of travel
Yes, because GPS devices always give functions
Yes, because time is always a valid input

quiz_questions.explanation: If the same time input gives two different position outputs, the relation fails the definition of a function. Each input must produce exactly one output.

quiz_questions.question_text: A temperature sensor records the temperature at every hour. At 3 PM it reads exactly 72°F. Could this temperature-time relationship be a function from time to temperature?

options.text: No, because different times could have the same temperature
Only if temperatures never repeat
No, because temperature is continuous
Yes, because at each specific time there is exactly one temperature reading

quiz_questions.explanation: At each moment in time (input), there is exactly one temperature (output). Different times may share the same temperature, but that does not prevent it from being a function.

quiz_questions.question_text: A social media platform maps each user to their number of followers. Is this a one-to-one function?

options.text: Yes, because each user has exactly one follower count
Yes, because each user has a unique number of followers
It is not a function at all
No, because multiple users can have the same follower count

quiz_questions.explanation: It is a function (each user has exactly one follower count), but not one-to-one because multiple users can share the same follower count.

quiz_questions.question_text: A parking garage charges a flat rate based on the number of hours parked: $5 for 0-2 hours, $10 for 2-4 hours, $15 for 4-6 hours. Is the cost a function of hours parked?

options.text: No, because different hour amounts give the same cost
Yes, because each number of hours yields exactly one cost
Only for exact hour amounts
No, because the cost is not continuous

quiz_questions.explanation: Each input (hours parked) maps to exactly one cost, making it a function. The fact that multiple hour values share the same cost does not violate the function definition.

quiz_questions.question_text: A phone's contact list maps each contact name to a phone number. If 'Mom' is stored with two different numbers, is this a function from names to numbers?

options.text: Yes, because contacts are always functions
It depends on which number is called first
No, because the input 'Mom' maps to two different outputs
Yes, because phone numbers are unique

quiz_questions.explanation: If one contact name (input) is associated with two different phone numbers (outputs), the relation is not a function because a function requires each input to have exactly one output.

quiz_questions.question_text: An airline assigns each flight number to exactly one departure gate. The airline also assigns different flights to the same gate at different times. Is the mapping from flight numbers to gates a function?

options.text: Only if gates are never shared
Yes, because each flight number maps to exactly one gate
It is a relation, not a function
No, because gates are shared

quiz_questions.explanation: Each flight number (input) maps to exactly one departure gate (output), satisfying the definition of a function. Multiple flights sharing a gate means it is not one-to-one, but it is still a function.

quiz_questions.question_text: A biologist records the height of a plant at various times. At week 3, the plant is measured at exactly 12 cm. The biologist claims this height-vs-time data represents a function. Which reasoning supports this claim?

options.text: The height increases over time
The data is recorded in a table
At each specific time, the plant has exactly one height
Plants always grow as functions

quiz_questions.explanation: At any given time, a plant has exactly one height, so the relationship from time to height satisfies the definition of a function.

quiz_questions.question_text: A store's return policy maps each receipt to a refund amount. If a receipt could potentially yield either a full refund or store credit (two possible outputs), is this mapping a function?

options.text: Yes, because store credit counts as a refund
It depends on the refund amount
No, because one input (receipt) could lead to two different outputs
Yes, because every receipt gets a refund

quiz_questions.explanation: If one receipt can lead to two different outcomes (full refund or store credit), the same input maps to multiple outputs, violating the definition of a function.
